  1092→    def check_epic_complete(self, parent_id: str) -> bool:
  1093→        """
  1094→        Check if all child issues of a epic are complete.
  1095→
  1096→        Args:
  1097→            parent_id: Parent epic issue ID
  1098→
  1099→        Returns:
  1100→            True if all children are done/finalized/canceled, False otherwise
  1101→        """
  1102→        cursor = self.conn.execute(
  1103→            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE parent_id = ? AND status NOT IN ('done', 'finalized', 'canceled')",
  1104→            (parent_id,),
  1105→        )
  1106→        return cursor.fetchone()[0] == 0
  1107→
  1108→    def get_model_performance(self, model: Optional[str] = None, tag: Optional[str] = None, group_by: str = "tag") -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
  1109→        """Get model performance stats, optionally filtered by model or tag.
  1110→
  1111→        Args:
  1112→            model: Filter to a specific model name.
  1113→            tag: Filter to issues containing this tag.
  1114→            group_by: "tag" (default) groups by model × tag, "type" groups by model × type.
  1115→
  1116→        Returns aggregated stats: model, group label, success/failure counts, retries, tokens, duration.
  1117→        """
  1118→        if group_by == "tag":
  1119→            group_col = "COALESCE(jt.value, 'untagged')"
  1120→            group_alias = "tag"
  1121→            from_clause = """FROM issues i
  1122→            LEFT JOIN json_each(i.tags) jt ON 1=1
  1123→            LEFT JOIN events e ON i.id = e.issue_id AND e.event_type = 'tokens_used'"""
  1124→        else:
  1125→            group_col = "i.type"
  1126→            group_alias = "type"
  1127→            from_clause = """FROM issues i
  1128→            LEFT JOIN events e ON i.id = e.issue_id AND e.event_type = 'tokens_used'"""
  1129→
  1130→        query = f"""
  1131→            SELECT
  1132→                COALESCE(i.model, 'unknown') as model,
  1133→                {group_col} as {group_alias},
  1134→                COUNT(DISTINCT i.id) as issue_count,
  1135→                SUM(CASE WHEN i.status IN ('done', 'finalized') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as successes,
  1136→                SUM(CASE WHEN i.status = 'failed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as failures,
  1137→                SUM(CASE WHEN i.status = 'escalated' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as escalations,
  1138→                (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events e2 WHERE e2.issue_id = i.id AND e2.event_type = 'retry') as total_retries,
  1139→                COALESCE(SUM(CAST(json_extract(e.detail, '$.input_tokens') AS INTEGER)), 0) as total_input_tokens,
  1140→                COALESCE(SUM(CAST(json_extract(e.detail, '$.output_tokens') AS INTEGER)), 0) as total_output_tokens,
  1141→                ROUND(AVG(
  1142→                    (julianday(COALESCE(i.closed_at, datetime('now'))) - julianday(i.created_at)) * 24 * 60
  1143→                ), 1) as avg_duration_minutes
  1144→            {from_clause}
  1145→            WHERE i.type != 'epic'
  1146→        """
  1147→        params: list = []
  1148→        if model:
  1149→            query += " AND i.model = ?"
  1150→            params.append(model)
  1151→        if tag:
  1152→            query += " AND i.tags LIKE ?"
  1153→            params.append(f'%"{tag}"%')
  1154→
  1155→        query += f" GROUP BY model, {group_alias} ORDER BY issue_count DESC"
  1156→
  1157→        cursor = self.conn.execute(query, params)
  1158→        return [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
  1159→
  1160→    def get_metrics(
  1161→        self,
  1162→        model: Optional[str] = None,
  1163→        tag: Optional[str] = None,
  1164→        issue_type: Optional[str] = None,
  1165→        project: Optional[str] = None,
  1166→    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
  1167→        """Get aggregated metrics from agent_runs view.
  1168→
  1169→        Args:
  1170→            model: Filter to a specific model name.
  1171→            tag: Filter to issues containing this tag.
  1172→            issue_type: Filter to a specific issue type.
  1173→            project: Filter to a specific project (optional).
  1174→
  1175→        Returns:
  1176→            List of dicts with aggregated metrics per model:
  1177→            - model: Model name
  1178→            - runs: Total number of runs
  1179→            - success_count: Number of successful runs
  1180→            - failed_count: Number of failed runs
  1181→            - escalated_count: Number of escalated runs
  1182→            - success_rate: Success percentage
  1183→            - avg_duration_s: Average duration in seconds
  1184→            - avg_retries: Average retry count
  1185→            - merge_health: Percentage of runs with clean merge (tests_passed / (tests_passed + test_failure + rebase_conflict))
  1186→        """
  1187→        if not self.conn:
  1188→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
  1189→
  1190→        # Join with issues table to filter by project
  1191→        from_clause = "FROM agent_runs ar"
  1192→        if project is not None:
  1193→            from_clause += " JOIN issues i ON ar.issue_id = i.id"
  1194→
  1195→        query = f"""
  1196→            SELECT
  1197→                ar.model,
  1198→                COUNT(*) as runs,
  1199→                SUM(CASE WHEN ar.outcome = 'done' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as success_count,
  1200→                SUM(CASE WHEN ar.outcome = 'failed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as failed_count,
  1201→                SUM(CASE WHEN ar.outcome = 'escalated' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as escalated_count,
  1202→                ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN ar.outcome = 'done' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) as success_rate,
  1203→                ROUND(AVG(ar.duration_s), 1) as avg_duration_s,
  1204→                ROUND(AVG(ar.retry_count), 1) as avg_retries
  1205→            {from_clause}
  1206→            WHERE 1=1
  1207→        """
  1208→        params: list = []
  1209→
  1210→        if project:
  1211→            query += " AND i.project = ?"
  1212→            params.append(project)
  1213→        if model:
  1214→            query += " AND ar.model = ?"
  1215→            params.append(model)
  1216→        if tag:
  1217→            query += " AND ar.tags LIKE ?"
  1218→            params.append(f'%"{tag}"%')
  1219→        if issue_type:
  1220→            query += " AND ar.issue_type = ?"
  1221→            params.append(issue_type)
  1222→
  1223→        query += " GROUP BY ar.model ORDER BY runs DESC"
  1224→
  1225→        cursor = self.conn.execute(query, params)
  1226→        results = [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
  1227→
  1228→        # Add merge health calculation per model
  1229→        for result in results:
  1230→            model_name = result["model"]
  1231→            # Count merge-related events for issues run by this model
  1232→            merge_from_clause = "FROM events e JOIN agent_runs ar ON e.issue_id = ar.issue_id"
  1233→            if project is not None:
  1234→                merge_from_clause += " JOIN issues i ON ar.issue_id = i.id"
  1235→
  1236→            merge_query = f"""
  1237→                SELECT
  1238→                    SUM(CASE WHEN e.event_type = 'tests_passed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as tests_passed,
  1239→                    SUM(CASE WHEN e.event_type = 'test_failure' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as test_failure,
  1240→                    SUM(CASE WHEN e.event_type = 'rebase_conflict' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as rebase_conflict
  1241→                {merge_from_clause}
  1242→                WHERE ar.model = ?
  1243→            """
  1244→            merge_params = [model_name]
  1245→            if project:
  1246→                merge_query += " AND i.project = ?"
  1247→                merge_params.append(project)
  1248→            if tag:
  1249→                merge_query += " AND ar.tags LIKE ?"
  1250→                merge_params.append(f'%"{tag}"%')
  1251→            if issue_type:
  1252→                merge_query += " AND ar.issue_type = ?"
  1253→                merge_params.append(issue_type)
  1254→
  1255→            cursor = self.conn.execute(merge_query, merge_params)
  1256→            merge_row = cursor.fetchone()
  1257→            tests_passed = merge_row["tests_passed"] or 0
  1258→            test_failure = merge_row["test_failure"] or 0
  1259→            rebase_conflict = merge_row["rebase_conflict"] or 0
  1260→            total_merge_events = tests_passed + test_failure + rebase_conflict
  1261→
  1262→            if total_merge_events > 0:
  1263→                result["merge_health"] = round(100.0 * tests_passed / total_merge_events, 1)
  1264→            else:
  1265→                result["merge_health"] = None
  1266→
  1267→        return results
  1268→

1→"""Human CLI interface for Hive orchestrator."""
     2→
     3→import argparse
     4→import json
     5→import os
     6→import shlex
     7→import subprocess
     8→import sys
     9→import time
    10→from pathlib import Path
    11→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
    12→
    13→# Set CLI context for logging configuration
    14→os.environ["HIVE_CLI_CONTEXT"] = "1"
    15→
    16→from .config import Config
    17→from .daemon import HiveDaemon
    18→from .db import Database, validate_tags
    19→from .git import GitWorktreeError, get_worktree_dirty_status
    20→from .utils import detect_project
    21→
    22→
    23→class HiveCLI:
    24→    """Command-line interface for Hive orchestrator."""
    25→
    26→    def __init__(self, db: Database, project_path: str):
    27→        self.db = db
    28→        self.project_path = Path(project_path).resolve()
    29→        self.project_name = self.project_path.name
    30→
    31→    def _error(self, msg: str, *, json_mode: bool = False):
    32→        """Print error and exit."""
    33→        if json_mode:
    34→            print(json.dumps({"error": msg}))
    35→        else:
    36→            print(f"Error: {msg}", file=sys.stderr)
    37→        sys.exit(1)
    38→
    39→    def _parse_tags(self, issue_dict: dict) -> dict:
    40→        """Parse tags JSON string into a list in-place."""
    41→        if issue_dict.get("tags"):
    42→            try:
    43→                issue_dict["tags"] = json.loads(issue_dict["tags"])
    44→            except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
    45→                issue_dict["tags"] = []
    46→        else:
    47→            issue_dict["tags"] = []
    48→        return issue_dict
    49→
    50→    # Map user-facing sort names to SQL column names
    51→    _SORT_COLUMNS = {
    52→        "priority": "priority",
    53→        "created": "created_at",
    54→        "updated": "updated_at",
    55→        "status": "status",
    56→        "title": "title",
    57→    }
    58→
    59→    # ── Issue management ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    60→
    61→    def create(
    62→        self,
    63→        title: str,
    64→        description: str = "",
    65→        priority: int = 2,
    66→        issue_type: str = "task",
    67→        model: Optional[str] = None,
    68→        tags: Optional[str] = None,
    69→        depends_on: Optional[list] = None,
    70→        *,
    71→        json_mode: bool = False,
    72→    ):
    73→        """Create a new issue."""
    74→        try:
    75→            tag_list = [t.strip() for t in tags.split(",")] if tags else None
    76→            issue_id = self.db.create_issue(
    77→                title=title,
    78→                description=description,
    79→                priority=priority,
    80→                issue_type=issue_type,
    81→                project=self.project_name,
    82→                model=model,
    83→                tags=tag_list,
    84→            )
    85→            # Wire dependencies immediately so the issue can't be claimed before they exist
    86→            deps_added = []
    87→            if depends_on:
    88→                for dep_id in depends_on:
    89→                    self.db.add_dependency(issue_id, dep_id, "blocks")
    90→                    deps_added.append(dep_id)
    91→
    92→            result = {
    93→                "issue_id": issue_id,
    94→                "title": title,
    95→                "status": "open",
    96→                "tags": tag_list or [],
    97→                "depends_on": deps_added,
    98→                "message": f"Created issue {issue_id}: {title}",
    99→            }
   100→        except Exception as e:
   101→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   102→
   103→        if json_mode:
   104→            print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   105→        else:
   106→            print(f"Created issue: {result['issue_id']}")
   107→            print(f"  Title: {title}")
   108→            print(f"  Priority: {priority}")
   109→            if result.get("tags"):
   110→                print(f"  Tags: {', '.join(result['tags'])}")
   111→            if depends_on:
   112→                print(f"  Depends on: {', '.join(depends_on)}")
   113→        return result.get("issue_id")
   114→
   115→    _DONE_STATUSES = ("done", "finalized", "canceled")
   116→
   117→    def list_issues(
   118→        self,
   119→        status: Optional[str] = None,
   120→        sort_by: str = "priority",
   121→        reverse: bool = False,
   122→        issue_type: Optional[str] = None,
   123→        assignee: Optional[str] = None,
   124→        limit: int = 50,
   125→        todo: bool = False,
   126→        *,
   127→        json_mode: bool = False,
   128→    ):
   129→        """List all issues."""
   130→        try:
   131→            query = "SELECT * FROM issues WHERE project = ?"
   132→            params: List[Any] = [self.project_name]
   133→
   134→            if todo:
   135→                placeholders = ",".join("?" for _ in self._DONE_STATUSES)
   136→                query += f" AND status NOT IN ({placeholders})"
   137→                params.extend(self._DONE_STATUSES)
   138→            elif status:
   139→                query += " AND status = ?"
   140→                params.append(status)
   141→            if assignee:
   142→                query += " AND assignee = ?"
   143→                params.append(assignee)
   144→            if issue_type:
   145→                query += " AND type = ?"
   146→                params.append(issue_type)
   147→
   148→            # Resolve sort column (default to priority if unknown)
   149→            sort_col = self._SORT_COLUMNS.get(sort_by, "priority")
   150→            direction = "DESC" if reverse else "ASC"
   151→            query += f" ORDER BY {sort_col} {direction}"
   152→
   153→            query += " LIMIT ?"
   154→            params.append(str(limit))
   155→
   156→            cursor = self.db.conn.execute(query, params)
   157→            issues = []
   158→            for row in cursor.fetchall():
   159→                issue_dict = dict(row)
   160→                self._parse_tags(issue_dict)
   161→                issues.append(issue_dict)
   162→
   163→            result = {"count": len(issues), "issues": issues}
   164→        except Exception as e:
   165→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   166→
   167→        if json_mode:
   168→            print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   169→        else:
   170→            issues = result.get("issues", [])
   171→            if not issues:
   172→                print("No issues found.")
   173→                print("  Create one with: hive create 'title' 'description'")
   174→                return
   175→            print(f"\n{'ID':<12} {'Status':<12} {'Pri':<4} {'Type':<10} {'Title':<40}")
   176→            print("-" * 80)
   177→            for issue in issues:
   178→                itype = issue.get("type", "")[:10]
   179→                print(f"{issue['id']:<12} {issue['status']:<12} {issue['priority']:<4} {itype:<10} {issue['title'][:40]}")
   180→            print(f"\nTotal: {len(issues)} issues")
   181→
   182→    def show(self, issue_id: str, *, json_mode: bool = False):
   183→        """Show issue details and events."""
   184→        try:
   185→            issue = self.db.get_issue(issue_id)
   186→            if not issue:
   187→                raise ValueError(f"Issue not found: {issue_id}")
   188→
   189→            # Get dependencies
   190→            cursor = self.db.conn.execute(
   191→                """
   192→                SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status
   193→                FROM dependencies d
   194→                JOIN issues i ON d.depends_on = i.id
   195→                WHERE d.issue_id = ?
   196→                """,
   197→                (issue_id,),
   198→            )
   199→            dependencies = [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   200→
   201→            # Get dependents (issues blocked by this one)
   202→            cursor = self.db.conn.execute(
   203→                """
   204→                SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status
   205→                FROM dependencies d
   206→                JOIN issues i ON d.issue_id = i.id
   207→                WHERE d.depends_on = ?
   208→                """,
   209→                (issue_id,),
   210→            )
   211→            dependents = [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   212→
   213→            # Get recent events
   214→            events = self.db.get_events(issue_id=issue_id, limit=10)
   215→
   216→            # Parse tags from JSON
   217→            issue_dict = dict(issue)
   218→            self._parse_tags(issue_dict)
   219→
   220→            result = {
   221→                "issue": issue_dict,
   222→                "dependencies": dependencies,
   223→                "dependents": dependents,
   224→                "recent_events": events,
   225→            }
   226→        except Exception as e:
   227→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   228→
   229→        if json_mode:
   230→            print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   231→        else:
   232→            issue = result["issue"]
   233→            print(f"\nIssue: {issue['id']}")
   234→            print(f"Title: {issue['title']}")
   235→            print(f"Status: {issue['status']}")
   236→            print(f"Priority: {issue['priority']}")
   237→            print(f"Type: {issue['type']}")
   238→            print(f"Assignee: {issue['assignee'] or 'None'}")
   239→            if issue.get("tags"):
   240→                print(f"Tags: {', '.join(issue['tags'])}")
   241→            if issue.get("model"):
   242→                print(f"Model: {issue['model']}")
   243→            print(f"Created: {issue['created_at']}")
   244→            if issue["description"]:
   245→                print(f"\nDescription:\n{issue['description']}")
   246→            deps = result.get("dependencies", [])
   247→            if deps:
   248→                print("\nDepends on:")
   249→                for dep in deps:
   250→                    print(f"  - {dep['id']}: {dep['title']} ({dep['status']})")
   251→            events = result.get("recent_events", [])
   252→            if events:
   253→                print(f"\nEvents ({len(events)}):")
   254→                for event in events[:10]:
   255→                    print(f"  [{event['created_at']}] {event['event_type']}")
   256→                    if event["detail"]:
   257→                        detail = json.loads(event["detail"]) if isinstance(event["detail"], str) else event["detail"]
   258→                        for key, value in detail.items():
   259→                            print(f"    {key}: {value}")
   260→
   261→    def update(
   262→        self,
   263→        issue_id: str,
   264→        title: Optional[str] = None,
   265→        description: Optional[str] = None,
   266→        priority: Optional[int] = None,
   267→        status: Optional[str] = None,
   268→        model: Optional[str] = None,
   269→        tags: Optional[str] = None,
   270→        *,
   271→        json_mode: bool = False,
   272→    ):
   273→        """Update an issue."""
   274→        try:
   275→            issue = self.db.get_issue(issue_id)
   276→            if not issue:
   277→                raise ValueError(f"Issue not found: {issue_id}")
   278→
   279→            tag_list = [t.strip() for t in tags.split(",")] if tags is not None else None
   280→
   281→            updates = []
   282→            params = []
   283→
   284→            if title is not None:
   285→                updates.append("title = ?")
   286→                params.append(title)
   287→            if description is not None:
   288→                updates.append("description = ?")
   289→                params.append(description)
   290→            if priority is not None:
   291→                updates.append("priority = ?")
   292→                params.append(priority)
   293→            if status is not None:
   294→                updates.append("status = ?")
   295→                params.append(status)
   296→            if model is not None:
   297→                updates.append("model = ?")
   298→                params.append(model)
   299→            if tag_list is not None:
   300→                validated_tags = validate_tags(tag_list)
   301→                updates.append("tags = ?")
   302→                params.append(json.dumps(validated_tags))
   303→
   304→            if updates:
   305→                query = f"UPDATE issues SET {', '.join(updates)}, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?"
   306→                params.append(issue_id)
   307→                self.db.conn.execute(query, params)
   308→                self.db.conn.commit()
   309→
   310→                self.db.log_event(
   311→                    issue_id,
   312→                    None,
   313→                    "updated",
   314→                    {
   315→                        "fields": [
   316→                            k
   317→                            for k, v in [
   318→                                ("title", title),
   319→                                ("description", description),
   320→                                ("priority", priority),
   321→                                ("status", status),
   322→                                ("model", model),
   323→                            ]
   324→                            if v is not None
   325→                        ]
   326→                    },
   327→                )
   328→
   329→            result = {"issue_id": issue_id, "message": f"Updated issue {issue_id}"}
   330→        except Exception as e:
   331→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   332→
   333→        if json_mode:
   334→            print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   335→        else:
   336→            print(result.get("message", f"Updated issue {issue_id}"))
   337→
   338→    def cancel(self, issue_id: str, reason: str = "", *, json_mode: bool = False):
   339→        """Cancel an issue."""
   340→        try:
   341→            issue = self.db.get_issue(issue_id)
   342→            if not issue:
   343→                raise ValueError(f"Issue not found: {issue_id}")
   344→
   345→            self.db.update_issue_status(issue_id, "canceled")
   346→            self.db.log_event(issue_id, None, "canceled", {"reason": reason})
   347→
   348→            result = {
   349→                "issue_id": issue_id,
   350→                "status": "canceled",
   351→                "reason": reason,
   352→                "message": f"Canceled issue {issue_id}",
   353→            }
   354→        except Exception as e:
   355→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   356→
   357→        if json_mode:
   358→            print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   359→        else:
   360→            print(result.get("message", f"Canceled issue {issue_id}"))
   361→
   362→    def finalize(self, issue_id: str, resolution: str = "", *, json_mode: bool = False):
   363→        """Finalize/close an issue."""
   364→        try:
   365→            issue = self.db.get_issue(issue_id)
   366→            if not issue:
   367→                raise ValueError(f"Issue not found: {issue_id}")
   368→
   369→            self.db.update_issue_status(issue_id, "finalized")
   370→            # If this issue was sitting in the merge queue (manual review mode),
   371→            # mark those entries complete so they don't get processed later.
   372→            self.db.conn.execute(
   373→                """
   374→                UPDATE merge_queue
   375→                SET status = 'merged', completed_at = datetime('now')
   376→                WHERE issue_id = ? AND status IN ('queued', 'running')
   377→                """,
   378→                (issue_id,),
   379→            )
   380→            self.db.conn.commit()
   381→            self.db.log_event(issue_id, None, "finalized", {"resolution": resolution})
   382→
   383→            result = {
   384→                "issue_id": issue_id,
   385→                "status": "finalized",
   386→                "resolution": resolution,
   387→                "message": f"Finalized issue {issue_id}",
   388→            }
   389→        except Exception as e:
   390→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   391→
   392→        if json_mode:
   393→            print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   394→        else:
   395→            print(result.get("message", f"Finalized issue {issue_id}"))
   396→
   397→    def review(self, limit: int = 20, *, json_mode: bool = False):
   398→        """List done issues that are pending finalization with review hints."""
   399→        try:
   400→            cursor = self.db.conn.execute(
   401→                """
   402→                SELECT
   403→                    i.id,
   404→                    i.title,
   405→                    i.updated_at,
   406→                    i.assignee,
   407→                    mq.status AS merge_status,
   408→                    mq.branch_name,
   409→                    mq.worktree,
   410→                    mq.enqueued_at
   411→                FROM issues i
   412→                LEFT JOIN merge_queue mq
   413→                    ON mq.id = (
   414→                        SELECT mq2.id
   415→                        FROM merge_queue mq2
   416→                        WHERE mq2.issue_id = i.id
   417→                        ORDER BY mq2.id DESC
   418→                        LIMIT 1
   419→                    )
   420→                WHERE i.project = ? AND i.status = 'done'
   421→                ORDER BY i.updated_at DESC
   422→                LIMIT ?
   423→                """,
   424→                (self.project_name, limit),
   425→            )
   426→
   427→            rows = []
   428→            project_q = shlex.quote(str(self.project_path))
   429→            for row in cursor.fetchall():
   430→                item = dict(row)
   431→                branch = item.get("branch_name")
   432→                worktree = item.get("worktree")
   433→                issue_id = item["id"]
   434→
   435→                item["diff_hint"] = None
   436→                item["merge_hint"] = None
   437→                item["finalize_hint"] = f'hive finalize {issue_id} --resolution "manual review complete"'
   438→
   439→                if branch:
   440→                    branch_q = shlex.quote(branch)
   441→                    item["diff_hint"] = f"git -C {project_q} diff main...{branch_q}"
   442→                    item["merge_hint"] = f"git -C {project_q} merge --ff-only {branch_q}"
   443→                if worktree:
   444→                    item["worktree_hint"] = f"git -C {shlex.quote(worktree)} log --oneline -n 5"
   445→                else:
   446→                    item["worktree_hint"] = None
   447→
   448→                rows.append(item)
   449→
   450→            result = {"count": len(rows), "review": rows}
   451→        except Exception as e:
   452→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   453→
   454→        if json_mode:
   455→            print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   456→            return
   457→
   458→        rows = result["review"]
   459→        if not rows:
   460→            print("No done issues pending review.")
   461→            return
   462→
   463→        print(f"\n{'Issue':<14} {'Merge':<10} {'Assignee':<16} {'Title':<40}")
   464→        print("-" * 88)
   465→        for item in rows:
   466→            merge_state = item.get("merge_status") or "-"
   467→            assignee = item.get("assignee") or "-"
   468→            print(f"{item['id']:<14} {merge_state:<10} {assignee:<16} {item['title'][:40]}")
   469→
   470→        print(f"\nTotal: {len(rows)} issue(s) pending finalization")
   471→        print("\nPer-issue review commands:")
   472→        for item in rows:
   473→            print(f"\n{item['id']}:")
   474→            if item.get("diff_hint"):
   475→                print(f"  Diff:     {item['diff_hint']}")
   476→            if item.get("worktree_hint"):
   477→                print(f"  Worktree: {item['worktree_hint']}")
   478→            if item.get("merge_hint"):
   479→                print(f"  Merge:    {item['merge_hint']}")
   480→            print(f"  Finalize: {item['finalize_hint']}")
   481→
   482→    def retry(self, issue_id: str, notes: str = "", *, json_mode: bool = False):
   483→        """Retry a failed/blocked issue."""
   484→        try:
   485→            issue = self.db.get_issue(issue_id)
   486→            if not issue:
   487→                raise ValueError(f"Issue not found: {issue_id}")
   488→
   489→            # Reset to open and unassign
   490→            self.db.conn.execute(
   491→                """
   492→                UPDATE issues
   493→                SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = datetime('now')
   494→                WHERE id = ?
   495→                """,
   496→                (issue_id,),
   497→            )
   498→            self.db.conn.commit()
   499→
   500→            self.db.log_event(issue_id, None, "manual_retry", {"notes": notes})
   501→
   502→            result = {
   503→                "issue_id": issue_id,
   504→                "status": "open",
   505→                "notes": notes,
   506→                "message": f"Reset issue {issue_id} to 'open' for retry",
   507→            }
   508→        except Exception as e:
   509→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   510→
   511→        if json_mode:
   512→            print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   513→        else:
   514→            print(result.get("message", f"Retrying issue {issue_id}"))
   515→
   516→    def epic(
   517→        self,
   518→        title: str,
   519→        description: str = "",
   520→        steps_json: str = "[]",
   521→        model: Optional[str] = None,
   522→        tags: Optional[str] = None,
   523→        *,
   524→        json_mode: bool = False,
   525→    ):
   526→        """Create a epic (multi-step workflow)."""
   527→        try:
   528→            steps = json.loads(steps_json)
   529→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
   530→            if json_mode:
   531→                print(json.dumps({"error": f"Invalid steps JSON: {e}"}))
   532→            else:
   533→                print(f"Error: Invalid steps JSON: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   534→            sys.exit(1)
   535→
   536→        try:
   537→            tag_list = [t.strip() for t in tags.split(",")] if tags else None
   538→
   539→            # Create parent epic issue
   540→            parent_id = self.db.create_issue(
   541→                title=title,
   542→                description=description,
   543→                issue_type="epic",
   544→                project=self.project_name,
   545→                tags=tag_list,
   546→            )
   547→
   548→            # Map of step indices to issue IDs
   549→            step_map: Dict[int, str] = {}
   550→            created_steps = []
   551→
   552→            # Create all step issues
   553→            for i, step in enumerate(steps):
   554→                step_id = self.db.create_issue(
   555→                    title=step["title"],
   556→                    description=step.get("description", ""),
   557→                    priority=step.get("priority", 2),
   558→                    issue_type="step",
   559→                    project=self.project_name,
   560→                    parent_id=parent_id,
   561→                    model=model,
   562→                )
   563→                step_map[i] = step_id
   564→                created_steps.append({"index": i, "id": step_id, "title": step["title"]})
   565→
   566→            # Wire up dependencies
   567→            for i, step in enumerate(steps):
   568→                needs = step.get("needs", [])
   569→                for dep_idx in needs:
   570→                    if isinstance(dep_idx, int) and dep_idx in step_map:
   571→                        self.db.add_dependency(step_map[i], step_map[dep_idx], "blocks")
   572→
   573→            result = {
   574→                "epic_id": parent_id,
   575→                "title": title,
   576→                "steps_count": len(steps),
   577→                "steps": created_steps,
   578→                "message": f"Created epic {parent_id} with {len(steps)} steps",
   579→            }
   580→        except Exception as e:
   581→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   582→
   583→        if json_mode:
   584→            print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   585→        else:
   586→            print(result.get("message", f"Created epic {result.get('epic_id', '')}"))
   587→            for step in result.get("steps", []):
   588→                print(f"  Step {step['index']}: {step['id']} - {step['title']}")
   589→
   590→    def dep_add(
   591→        self,
   592→        issue_id: str,
   593→        depends_on: str,
   594→        dep_type: str = "blocks",
   595→        *,
   596→        json_mode: bool = False,
   597→    ):
   598→        """Add a dependency between issues."""
   599→        try:
   600→            # Verify both issues exist
   601→            if not self.db.get_issue(issue_id):
   602→                raise ValueError(f"Issue not found: {issue_id}")
   603→            if not self.db.get_issue(depends_on):
   604→                raise ValueError(f"Dependency not found: {depends_on}")
   605→
   606→            self.db.add_dependency(issue_id, depends_on, dep_type)
   607→
   608→            result = {
   609→                "issue_id": issue_id,
   610→                "depends_on": depends_on,
   611→                "type": dep_type,
   612→                "message": f"Added {dep_type} dependency: {issue_id} depends on {depends_on}",
   613→            }
   614→        except Exception as e:
   615→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   616→
   617→        if json_mode:
   618→            print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   619→        else:
   620→            print(result.get("message", "Added dependency"))
   621→
   622→    def dep_remove(self, issue_id: str, depends_on: str, *, json_mode: bool = False):
   623→        """Remove a dependency between issues."""
   624→        try:
   625→            self.db.conn.execute(
   626→                "DELETE FROM dependencies WHERE issue_id = ? AND depends_on = ?",
   627→                (issue_id, depends_on),
   628→            )
   629→            self.db.conn.commit()
   630→
   631→            result = {
   632→                "issue_id": issue_id,
   633→                "depends_on": depends_on,
   634→                "message": f"Removed dependency: {issue_id} no longer depends on {depends_on}",
   635→            }
   636→        except Exception as e:
   637→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   638→
   639→        if json_mode:
   640→            print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   641→        else:
   642→            print(result.get("message", "Removed dependency"))

   644→    def merges(self, status: Optional[str] = None, *, json_mode: bool = False):
   645→        """List merge queue entries."""
   646→        query = "SELECT mq.*, i.title as issue_title, a.name as agent_name FROM merge_queue mq JOIN issues i ON mq.issue_id = i.id LEFT JOIN agents a ON mq.agent_id = a.id WHERE i.project = ?"
   647→        params = [self.project_name]
   648→        if status:
   649→            query += " AND mq.status = ?"
   650→            params.append(status)
   651→        query += " ORDER BY mq.enqueued_at DESC LIMIT 50"
   652→
   653→        cursor = self.db.conn.execute(query, params)
   654→        entries = [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   655→
   656→        # Compute per-status counts from the result set
   657→        status_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
   658→        for e in entries:
   659→            s = e.get("status") or "unknown"
   660→            status_counts[s] = status_counts.get(s, 0) + 1
   661→
   662→        if json_mode:
   663→            print(json.dumps({"count": len(entries), "status_counts": status_counts, "merges": entries}, indent=2, default=str))
   664→        else:
   665→            if not entries:
   666→                print("No merge queue entries found.")
   667→                return
   668→            print(f"\n{'ID':<6} {'Status':<10} {'Issue':<14} {'Title':<30} {'Branch':<25} {'Enqueued'}")
   669→            print("-" * 100)
   670→            for e in entries:
   671→                title = (e.get("issue_title") or "")[:30]
   672→                branch = (e.get("branch_name") or "")[:25]
   673→                print(f"{e['id']:<6} {e['status']:<10} {e['issue_id']:<14} {title:<30} {branch:<25} {e.get('enqueued_at', '')}")
   674→            summary_parts = [f"{count} {s}" for s, count in status_counts.items() if count > 0]
   675→            print(f"\n{', '.join(summary_parts)}")
   676→
   677→    def status(self, *, json_mode: bool = False):
   678→        """Show orchestrator status."""
   679→        try:
   680→            # Count issues by status
   681→            cursor = self.db.conn.execute(
   682→                """
   683→                SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count
   684→                FROM issues
   685→                WHERE project = ?
   686→                GROUP BY status
   687→                """,
   688→                (self.project_name,),
   689→            )
   690→            status_counts = {row[0]: row[1] for row in cursor.fetchall()}
   691→
   692→            # Get active agents with issue titles
   693→            active_agents = self.db.get_active_agents(project=self.project_name)
   694→            workers_detail = []
   695→            for agent in active_agents:
   696→                issue_title = ""
   697→                if agent.get("current_issue"):
   698→                    issue_row = self.db.get_issue(agent["current_issue"])
   699→                    if issue_row:
   700→                        issue_title = issue_row.get("title", "")
   701→                workers_detail.append(
   702→                    {
   703→                        "name": agent.get("name", ""),
   704→                        "issue_id": agent.get("current_issue", ""),
   705→                        "issue_title": issue_title,
   706→                    }
   707→                )
   708→
   709→            # Get running merge entry (refinery status proxy)
   710→            try:
   711→                cursor = self.db.conn.execute(
   712→                    """
   713→                    SELECT mq.issue_id, i.title as issue_title
   714→                    FROM merge_queue mq
   715→                    LEFT JOIN issues i ON mq.issue_id = i.id
   716→                    WHERE mq.status = 'running'
   717→                    LIMIT 1
   718→                    """
   719→                )
   720→                running_merge = cursor.fetchone()
   721→                refinery_info = {
   722→                    "active": running_merge is not None,
   723→                    "issue_id": running_merge["issue_id"] if running_merge else None,
   724→                    "issue_title": running_merge["issue_title"] if running_merge else None,
   725→                }
   726→            except Exception:
   727→                refinery_info = {"active": False, "issue_id": None, "issue_title": None}
   728→
   729→            # Get ready queue
   730→            ready = self.db.get_ready_queue(limit=10)
   731→
   732→            # Get merge queue stats
   733→            merge_stats = self.db.get_merge_queue_stats()
   734→
   735→            # Merge preflight visibility: report when dirty main worktree blocks merges.
   736→            main_worktree = {
   737→                "dirty": False,
   738→                "changes": [],
   739→                "status": "clean",
   740→            }
   741→            merge_blockers: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   742→            try:
   743→                dirty, dirty_output = get_worktree_dirty_status(str(self.project_path))
   744→                if dirty:
   745→                    changes = dirty_output.splitlines()
   746→                    main_worktree = {
   747→                        "dirty": True,
   748→                        "changes": changes[:20],
   749→                        "status": "dirty",
   750→                    }
   751→                    if merge_stats.get("queued", 0) > 0 or merge_stats.get("running", 0) > 0:
   752→                        merge_blockers.append(
   753→                            {
   754→                                "type": "dirty_main_worktree",
   755→                                "message": "Merges are paused: main worktree has uncommitted tracked changes",
   756→                                "changes": changes[:20],
   757→                            }
   758→                        )
   759→            except GitWorktreeError as e:
   760→                main_worktree = {
   761→                    "dirty": False,
   762→                    "changes": [],
   763→                    "status": "error",
   764→                    "error": str(e),
   765→                }
   766→
   767→            # Get daemon status
   768→            daemon = self._make_daemon()
   769→            daemon_status = daemon.status()
   770→
   771→            result = {
   772→                "project": self.project_name,
   773→                "issues": status_counts,
   774→                "total_issues": sum(status_counts.values()),
   775→                "active_agents": len(active_agents),
   776→                "workers": workers_detail,
   777→                "refinery": refinery_info,
   778→                "ready_queue": len(ready),
   779→                "merge_queue": merge_stats,
   780→                "merge_blockers": merge_blockers,
   781→                "main_worktree": main_worktree,
   782→                "ready_issues": [{"id": i["id"], "title": i["title"]} for i in ready[:5]],
   783→                "daemon": {
   784→                    "running": daemon_status.get("running", False),
   785→                    "pid": daemon_status.get("pid"),
   786→                    "log_file": daemon_status.get("log_file"),
   787→                },
   788→            }
   789→        except Exception as e:
   790→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   791→
   792→        if json_mode:
   793→            print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   794→        else:
   795→            print("\n=== Hive Status ===")
   796→            print(f"\nProject: {result.get('project', self.project_name)}")
   797→            print("\nIssues:")
   798→            for s in [
   799→                "open",
   800→                "in_progress",
   801→                "done",
   802→                "finalized",
   803→                "failed",
   804→                "blocked",
   805→                "canceled",
   806→            ]:
   807→                count = result.get("issues", {}).get(s, 0)
   808→                if count > 0:
   809→                    print(f"  {s}: {count}")
   810→            print(f"\nActive workers: {result.get('active_agents', 0)}/{Config.MAX_AGENTS}")
   811→            for w in result.get("workers", []):
   812→                title = (w.get("issue_title") or "")[:40]
   813→                print(f"  {w.get('name', ''):<24} {w.get('issue_id', ''):<16} {title}")
   814→
   815→            refinery = result.get("refinery", {})
   816→            if refinery.get("active"):
   817→                rtitle = (refinery.get("issue_title") or "")[:40]
   818→                print(f"\nRefinery: reviewing {refinery.get('issue_id', '')} ({rtitle})")
   819→            else:
   820→                print("\nRefinery: idle")
   821→
   822→            print(f"Ready queue: {result.get('ready_queue', 0)} issues")
   823→            mq = result.get("merge_queue", {})
   824→            if isinstance(mq, dict):
   825→                parts = []
   826→                for k in ["queued", "running", "merged", "failed"]:
   827→                    v = mq.get(k, 0)
   828→                    if v > 0:
   829→                        parts.append(f"{v} {k}")
   830→                print(f"Merge queue: {', '.join(parts) if parts else 'empty'}")
   831→            else:
   832→                print(f"Merge queue: {mq} pending")
   833→
   834→            blockers = result.get("merge_blockers", [])
   835→            if blockers:
   836→                print("\nMerge blockers:")
   837→                for blocker in blockers:
   838→                    print(f"  - {blocker.get('message', blocker.get('type', 'unknown blocker'))}")
   839→                    changes = blocker.get("changes") or []
   840→                    for line in changes[:5]:
   841→                        print(f"    {line}")
   842→
   843→            # Daemon info
   844→            daemon_info = result.get("daemon", {})
   845→            if daemon_info.get("running"):
   846→                print(f"\nDaemon: running (PID {daemon_info.get('pid')})")
   847→                if daemon_info.get("log_file"):
   848→                    print(f"  Log: {daemon_info.get('log_file')}")
   849→            else:
   850→                print("\nDaemon: not running")
   851→
   852→            total = result.get("total_issues", 0)
   853→            if total == 0:
   854→                print("\n  No issues yet. Create one with: hive create 'title' 'description'")
   855→
   856→    def list_agents(self, agent_id: Optional[str] = None, status: Optional[str] = None, *, json_mode: bool = False):
   857→        """List agents, or show details for a specific agent if agent_id is provided."""
   858→        # If agent_id is provided, show that agent's details
   859→        if agent_id:
   860→            try:
   861→                cursor = self.db.conn.execute("SELECT * FROM agents WHERE id = ?", (agent_id,))
   862→                row = cursor.fetchone()
   863→                if not row:
   864→                    raise ValueError(f"Agent not found: {agent_id}")
   865→
   866→                agent = dict(row)
   867→
   868→                # Get current issue details
   869→                if agent.get("current_issue"):
   870→                    issue = self.db.get_issue(agent["current_issue"])
   871→                    agent["current_issue_details"] = issue
   872→
   873→                # Get recent events for this agent
   874→                agent["recent_events"] = self.db.get_events(agent_id=agent_id, limit=10)
   875→
   876→                result = agent
   877→            except Exception as e:
   878→                self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   879→
   880→            if json_mode:
   881→                print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
   882→            else:
   883→                print(f"\nAgent: {result.get('id', agent_id)}")
   884→                print(f"Name: {result.get('name', '')}")
   885→                print(f"Status: {result.get('status', '')}")
   886→                if result.get("current_issue"):
   887→                    print(f"Current issue: {result['current_issue']}")
   888→                events = result.get("recent_events", [])
   889→                if events:
   890→                    print(f"\nRecent events ({len(events)}):")
   891→                    for event in events[:5]:
   892→                        print(f"  [{event['created_at']}] {event['event_type']}")
   893→            return
   894→
   895→        # Otherwise, list all agents
   896→        try:
   897→            query = "SELECT * FROM agents WHERE project = ?"
   898→            params = [self.project_name]
   899→
   900→            if status:
   901→                query += " AND status = ?"
   902→                params.append(status)
   903→
   904→            query += " ORDER BY created_at DESC"
   905→
   906→            cursor = self.db.conn.execute(query, params)
   907→            agents = [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   908→
   909→            # Enrich with current issue info
   910→            for agent in agents:
   911→                if agent.get("current_issue"):
   912→                    issue = self.db.get_issue(agent["current_issue"])
   913→                    if issue:
   914→                        agent["current_issue_title"] = issue.get("title", "unknown")
   915→
   916→            result = {"count": len(agents), "agents": agents}
   917→        except Exception as e:
   918→            self._error(str(e), json_mode=json_mode)
   919→

   924→            if not agents:

   929→            for agent in agents:
   930→                issue_title = agent.get("current_issue_title", agent.get("current_issue", "")) or "-"
   931→                print(f"{agent['id']:<16} {agent['name']:<16} {agent['status']:<10} {str(issue_title)[:30]}")

   962→    # ── Event log (tail-style, not tool-backed) ─────────────────────
   963→
   964→    def _format_event(self, event: dict) -> str:
   965→        """Format a single event as a log line."""
   966→        ts = event["created_at"]
   967→        etype = event["event_type"]
   968→        issue = event["issue_id"] or "-"
   969→        agent = event["agent_id"] or "-"
   970→
   971→        line = f"{ts}  {etype:<24s}  issue={issue:<10s}  agent={agent:<10s}"
   972→
   973→        if event["detail"]:
   974→            try:
   975→                detail = json.loads(event["detail"])
   976→                parts = [f"{k}={v}" for k, v in detail.items()]
   977→                line += "  " + " ".join(parts)
   978→            except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
   979→                line += f"  {event['detail']}"
   980→
   981→        return line
   982→
   983→    @staticmethod
   984→    def _event_to_json(event: dict) -> dict:
   985→        """Prepare an event dict for JSON serialisation.
   986→
   987→        Parses the ``detail`` field from a JSON string into a real object
   988→        so the output is a proper nested structure rather than an escaped
   989→        string.
   990→        """
   991→        out = dict(event)
   992→        if out.get("detail"):
   993→            try:
   994→                out["detail"] = json.loads(out["detail"]) if isinstance(out["detail"], str) else out["detail"]
   995→            except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
   996→                pass  # keep as-is
   997→        return out
   998→
   999→    def logs(
  1000→        self,
  1001→        follow: bool = False,
  1002→        n: int = 20,
  1003→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
  1004→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
  1005→        event_type: Optional[str] = None,
  1006→        daemon: bool = False,
  1007→        *,
  1008→        json_mode: bool = False,
  1009→    ):
  1010→        """Show event log, optionally tailing for new events."""
  1011→        # If --daemon flag is set, show daemon logs instead of event logs
  1012→        if daemon:
  1013→            daemon_obj = self._make_daemon()
  1014→            daemon_obj.logs(lines=n, follow=follow)
  1015→            return
  1016→
  1017→        recent = self.db.get_recent_events(n=n, issue_id=issue_id, agent_id=agent_id, event_type=event_type)
  1018→
  1019→        if json_mode:
  1020→            if follow:
  1021→                # Streaming JSON: one object per line (JSONL) so callers
  1022→                # can consume incrementally.
  1023→                for event in recent:
  1024→                    print(json.dumps(self._event_to_json(event), default=str))
  1025→
  1026→                cursor = recent[-1]["id"] if recent else self.db.get_max_event_id()
  1027→                try:
  1028→                    while True:
  1029→                        time.sleep(0.5)
  1030→                        new_events = self.db.get_events_since(after_id=cursor, issue_id=issue_id, agent_id=agent_id, event_type=event_type)
  1031→                        for event in new_events:
  1032→                            print(json.dumps(self._event_to_json(event), default=str))
  1033→                            cursor = event["id"]
  1034→                except KeyboardInterrupt:
  1035→                    pass
  1036→            else:
  1037→                events = [self._event_to_json(e) for e in recent]
  1038→                print(json.dumps(events, default=str))
  1039→            return
  1040→
  1041→        for event in recent:
  1042→            print(self._format_event(event))
  1043→
  1044→        if not follow:
  1045→            return
  1046→
  1047→        cursor = recent[-1]["id"] if recent else self.db.get_max_event_id()
  1048→
  1049→        try:
  1050→            while True:
  1051→                time.sleep(0.5)
  1052→                new_events = self.db.get_events_since(after_id=cursor, issue_id=issue_id, agent_id=agent_id, event_type=event_type)
  1053→                for event in new_events:
  1054→                    print(self._format_event(event))
  1055→                    cursor = event["id"]
  1056→        except KeyboardInterrupt:
  1057→            pass

  1069→    def doctor(self, fix: bool = False, *, json_mode: bool = False):
  1070→        """Run system health checks."""
  1071→        from .doctor import run_all_checks
  1072→
  1073→        results = run_all_checks(self.db)
  1074→
  1075→        # Apply fixes if requested
  1076→        fixed_checks = []
  1077→        if fix:
  1078→            for result in results:
  1079→                if result.status in ("fail", "warn") and result.fix is not None:
  1080→                    try:
  1081→                        result.fix(self.db)
  1082→                        fixed_checks.append(result.id)
  1083→                    except Exception as e:
  1084→                        if json_mode:
  1085→                            print(json.dumps({"error": f"Failed to fix {result.id}: {e}"}), file=sys.stderr)
  1086→                        else:
  1087→                            print(f"Error fixing {result.id}: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
  1088→
  1089→        if json_mode:
  1090→            output = [
  1091→                {
  1092→                    "id": r.id,
  1093→                    "status": r.status,
  1094→                    "description": r.description,
  1095→                    "details": r.details,
  1096→                    "fixable": r.fix is not None,
  1097→                    "fixed": r.id in fixed_checks,
  1098→                }
  1099→                for r in results
  1100→            ]
  1101→            print(json.dumps(output, indent=2))
  1102→            return
  1103→
  1104→        # Table output
  1105→        print(f"\n{'ID':<8} {'Status':<8} {'Description':<60}")
  1106→        print("-" * 76)
  1107→        for result in results:
  1108→            status_display = result.status.upper()
  1109→            description = result.description[:60]
  1110→            if result.id in fixed_checks:
  1111→                description += " [FIXED]"
  1112→            print(f"{result.id:<8} {status_display:<8} {description}")
  1113→
  1114→        # Summary
  1115→        failures = sum(1 for r in results if r.status == "fail")
  1116→        warnings = sum(1 for r in results if r.status == "warn")
  1117→
  1118→        print()
  1119→        if fix and fixed_checks:
  1120→            print(f"Fixed {len(fixed_checks)} check(s): {', '.join(fixed_checks)}")
  1121→        if failures > 0 or warnings > 0:
  1122→            parts = []
  1123→            if failures > 0:
  1124→                parts.append(f"{failures} failure(s)")
  1125→            if warnings > 0:
  1126→                parts.append(f"{warnings} warning(s)")
  1127→            print(f"Summary: {', '.join(parts)}")
  1128→        else:
  1129→            print("Summary: All checks passed")

  1270→    # ── Daemon management ────────────────────────────────────────────

  1272→    def _make_daemon(self) -> HiveDaemon:
  1273→        return HiveDaemon(self.project_name, str(self.project_path))
  1274→
  1275→    def start(self, foreground: bool = False, *, json_mode: bool = False):
  1276→        """Start the hive daemon."""
  1277→        if foreground:
  1278→            from .daemon import run_daemon_foreground
  1279→
  1280→            run_daemon_foreground(self.db, str(self.project_path), self.project_name)
  1281→            return
  1282→
  1283→        daemon = self._make_daemon()
  1284→        status = daemon.status()
  1285→        if status["running"]:
  1286→            if json_mode:
  1287→                print(json.dumps({"status": "already_running", "pid": status["pid"]}))
  1288→            else:
  1289→                print(f"Hive daemon already running (PID {status['pid']})")
  1290→            return
  1291→
  1292→        started = daemon.start(db_path=self.db.db_path)
  1293→        if started:
  1294→            ds = daemon.status()
  1295→            if json_mode:
  1296→                print(json.dumps({"status": "started", "pid": ds["pid"], "log_file": ds.get("log_file")}))
  1297→            else:
  1298→                print(f"Hive daemon started (PID {ds['pid']})")
  1299→                print(f"  Log: {ds.get('log_file')}")
  1300→        else:
  1301→            log_tail = ""
  1302→            try:
  1303→                if daemon.log_file.exists():
  1304→                    lines = daemon.log_file.read_text().strip().splitlines()
  1305→                    log_tail = "\n".join(lines[-10:])
  1306→            except OSError:
  1307→                pass
  1308→            if json_mode:
  1309→                print(json.dumps({"error": "Failed to start daemon", "log_file": str(daemon.log_file), "log_tail": log_tail}))
  1310→                sys.exit(1)
  1311→            else:
  1312→                print(f"Failed to start daemon. Log: {daemon.log_file}")
  1313→                if log_tail:
  1314→                    print(f"\nLast output:\n{log_tail}")
  1315→
  1316→    def stop(self, *, json_mode: bool = False):
  1317→        """Stop the hive daemon."""
  1318→        daemon = self._make_daemon()
  1319→        status = daemon.status()
  1320→        if not status["running"]:
  1321→            if json_mode:
  1322→                print(json.dumps({"status": "not_running"}))
  1323→            else:
  1324→                print("Hive daemon is not running.")
  1325→            return
  1326→        pid = status["pid"]
  1327→        stopped = daemon.stop()
  1328→        if stopped:
  1329→            if json_mode:
  1330→                print(json.dumps({"status": "stopped"}))
  1331→            else:
  1332→                print(f"Hive daemon stopped (was PID {pid})")
  1333→        else:
  1334→            if json_mode:
  1335→                print(json.dumps({"error": f"Failed to stop daemon (PID {pid})"}))
  1336→                sys.exit(1)
  1337→            else:
  1338→                print(f"Failed to stop daemon (PID {pid})")
  1339→

  1342→    def queen(self, *, backend: str | None = None):
  1343→        """Launch Queen Bee TUI using the configured backend."""
  1344→        daemon = self._make_daemon()
  1345→        daemon_status = daemon.status()
  1346→        if not daemon_status["running"]:
  1347→            print("Starting daemon... ", end="", flush=True)
  1348→            daemon.start(db_path=self.db.db_path)
  1349→            daemon_status = daemon.status()
  1350→            if daemon_status["running"]:
  1351→                print(f"done (PID {daemon_status['pid']})")
  1352→            else:
  1353→                print("failed")
  1354→                self._error("Failed to start daemon. Check `hive daemon logs`.")
  1355→
  1356→        effective = backend or Config.BACKEND
  1357→        if effective == "claude":
  1358→            self._queen_claude()
  1359→        elif effective == "codex":
  1360→            self._queen_codex()
  1361→        else:
  1362→            self._queen_opencode()

  1383→    def _queen_write_opencode_agent(self):
  1384→        """Generate .opencode/agents/queen.md from source template."""
  1385→        from .prompts import _load_template
  1386→
  1387→        queen_prompt = _load_template("queen")
  1388→        agents_dir = self.project_path / ".opencode" / "agents"

  1393→    def _queen_opencode(self):
  1394→        """Launch Queen Bee via OpenCode TUI."""
  1395→        self._queen_write_opencode_agent()
  1396→
  1397→        opencode_cmd = os.environ.get("OPENCODE_CMD", "opencode")
  1398→        cmd = [

  1400→            "attach",
  1401→            Config.OPENCODE_URL,
  1402→            "--dir",
  1403→            str(self.project_path),
  1404→        ]
  1405→
  1406→        print("Launching Queen Bee TUI (OpenCode)...\n")
  1407→        os.execvp(cmd[0], cmd)

  1417→        """
  1418→        from .prompts import _load_template
  1419→
  1420→        queen_prompt = _load_template("queen")
  1421→
  1422→        # Write full instructions to .hive/ so Queen can re-read after compaction
  1423→        hive_dir = self.project_path / ".hive"
  1424→        hive_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
  1425→        instructions_path = hive_dir / "queen-instructions.md"
  1426→        instructions_path.write_text(queen_prompt)
  1427→
  1428→        # Write condensed identity anchor to .claude/CLAUDE.md (re-read every turn)
  1429→        claude_dir = self.project_path / ".claude"
  1430→        claude_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
  1431→        claude_md = claude_dir / "CLAUDE.md"
  1432→
  1433→        queen_block = (
  1434→            f"\n{self._QUEEN_SENTINEL_START}\n"
  1435→            "# HIVE QUEEN BEE — ACTIVE SESSION\n"
  1436→            "You are the Queen Bee coordinator. You do NOT write code — you plan, decompose, and monitor.\n"
  1437→            "Full instructions: `.hive/queen-instructions.md` — re-read if your context feels incomplete.\n"
  1438→            "Operational state: `.hive/queen-state.md` — re-read to recall what you were working on.\n"
  1439→            "Always use `hive --json` for CLI commands. The daemon runs in background.\n"
  1440→            f"{self._QUEEN_SENTINEL_END}\n"
  1441→        )
  1442→
  1443→        existing = claude_md.read_text() if claude_md.exists() else ""
  1444→        if self._QUEEN_SENTINEL_START not in existing:
  1445→            claude_md.write_text(existing + queen_block)
  1446→
  1447→        return claude_md, instructions_path
  1448→
  1449→    def _queen_cleanup_identity_files(self, claude_md: Path, instructions_path: Path):
  1450→        """Remove Queen identity files written for the session."""
  1451→        # Strip queen block from CLAUDE.md
  1452→        if claude_md.exists():
  1453→            content = claude_md.read_text()
  1454→            start = content.find(self._QUEEN_SENTINEL_START)
  1455→            if start != -1:
  1456→                end = content.find(self._QUEEN_SENTINEL_END)
  1457→                if end != -1:
  1458→                    end += len(self._QUEEN_SENTINEL_END)
  1459→                    # Consume trailing newline if present
  1460→                    if end < len(content) and content[end] == "\n":
  1461→                        end += 1
  1462→                    cleaned = (content[:start] + content[end:]).rstrip("\n")
  1463→                    if cleaned.strip():
  1464→                        claude_md.write_text(cleaned + "\n")
  1465→                    else:
  1466→                        claude_md.unlink()
  1467→
  1468→        # Remove instructions file
  1469→        instructions_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
  1470→
  1471→        # Remove state file (ephemeral per-session)
  1472→        state_file = self.project_path / ".hive" / "queen-state.md"
  1473→        state_file.unlink(missing_ok=True)
  1474→
  1475→    def _queen_claude(self):
  1476→        """Launch Queen Bee as an interactive Claude CLI session."""
  1477→        # Claude CLI refuses to launch inside another Claude Code session.
  1478→        # Since the queen is a top-level interactive session (not nested), clear the guard.
  1479→        os.environ.pop("CLAUDECODE", None)
  1480→
  1481→        # Write identity files for compaction persistence
  1482→        claude_md, instructions_path = self._queen_write_identity_files()
  1483→
  1484→        # Short system prompt — full instructions are in the file
  1485→        short_prompt = "You are the Hive Queen Bee coordinator. Read .hive/queen-instructions.md for your full instructions now."
  1486→
  1487→        claude_cmd = os.environ.get("CLAUDE_CMD", "claude")
  1488→        cmd = [
  1489→            claude_cmd,
  1490→            "--model",
  1491→            Config.DEFAULT_MODEL,
  1492→            "--append-system-prompt",
  1493→            short_prompt,
  1494→            "--allowedTools",
  1495→            "Bash(hive:*) Bash(git:*) Bash(ls:*) Bash(find:*) Bash(rg:*) Read Edit Write",
  1496→        ]
  1497→
  1498→        print("Launching Queen Bee TUI (Claude CLI)...\n")
  1499→
  1500→        # Use subprocess.run (not os.execvp) so we can clean up identity files on exit
  1501→        try:
  1502→            result = subprocess.run(cmd)
  1503→            sys.exit(result.returncode)
  1504→        except KeyboardInterrupt:
  1505→            pass
  1506→        finally:
  1507→            self._queen_cleanup_identity_files(claude_md, instructions_path)
  1508→
  1509→    def _queen_codex(self):
  1510→        """Launch Queen Bee as an interactive Codex CLI session."""
  1511→        # Write identity files for compaction persistence (instructions + state files).
  1512→        claude_md, instructions_path = self._queen_write_identity_files()
  1513→
  1514→        short_prompt = "Read .hive/queen-instructions.md for your full instructions now."
  1515→
  1516→        # Persistent instructions that survive long chats + compaction.
  1517→        # Keep this short and point at the durable instruction/state files.
  1518→        developer_instructions = (
  1519→            "You are the Hive Queen Bee coordinator. You do NOT write code; you plan, decompose, and monitor.\\n"
  1520→            "Full instructions: .hive/queen-instructions.md (read now; re-read after compaction).\\n"
  1521→            "Operational state: .hive/queen-state.md (re-read after compaction; update after significant actions).\\n"
  1522→            "Before creating issues/epics, output a human-readable plan for user review and wait for explicit approval.\\n"
  1523→            "Always use hive --json for Hive CLI commands."
  1524→        )
  1525→
  1526→        # Codex compaction prompt (used when the client compacts long threads).
  1527→        # Ensure summaries keep the queen's durable context pointers.
  1528→        compact_prompt = (
  1529→            "Summarize the conversation for continuity.\\n"
  1530→            "Preserve: user goals, key decisions, current plan/issues, and next steps.\\n"
  1531→            "Always include a reminder to read .hive/queen-instructions.md and .hive/queen-state.md after compaction."
  1532→        )
  1533→
  1534→        codex_cmd = os.environ.get("CODEX_CMD", "codex")
  1535→        sandbox = os.environ.get("HIVE_CODEX_QUEEN_SANDBOX") or getattr(Config, "CODEX_SANDBOX", "workspace-write")
  1536→        approval = os.environ.get("HIVE_CODEX_QUEEN_APPROVAL_POLICY") or getattr(Config, "CODEX_APPROVAL_POLICY", "never")
  1537→        cmd = [
  1538→            codex_cmd,
  1539→            "--sandbox",
  1540→            sandbox,
  1541→            "--ask-for-approval",
  1542→            approval,
  1543→            "-c",
  1544→            f'developer_instructions="{developer_instructions}"',
  1545→            "-c",
  1546→            f'compact_prompt="{compact_prompt}"',
  1547→            "--cd",
  1548→            str(self.project_path),
  1549→            short_prompt,
  1550→        ]
  1551→
  1552→        print("Launching Queen Bee TUI (Codex CLI)...\n")
  1553→
  1554→        try:
  1555→            result = subprocess.run(cmd)
  1556→            sys.exit(result.returncode)
  1557→        except FileNotFoundError:
  1558→            self._error("Codex CLI not found. Install `codex` and ensure it's on PATH, or set CODEX_CMD to the codex executable path.")
  1559→        except KeyboardInterrupt:
  1560→            pass
  1561→        finally:
  1562→            self._queen_cleanup_identity_files(claude_md, instructions_path)
  1563→

  1565→def _do_setup(project_path: Path, project_name: str, *, json_mode: bool = False):
  1566→    """Write a default .hive.toml if one doesn't exist."""
  1567→    target = project_path / ".hive.toml"
  1568→    if target.exists():
  1569→        if json_mode:
  1570→            print(json.dumps({"config_exists": True, "path": str(target)}))
  1571→        else:
  1572→            print(f"{target} already exists.")
  1573→        return
  1574→    target.write_text(f'[project]\nname = "{project_name}"\n\n[hive]\nbackend = "claude"\nmerge_queue_enabled = false\n')
  1575→    if json_mode:
  1576→        print(json.dumps({"config_created": str(target)}))
  1577→    else:
  1578→        print(f"Created {target}")
  1579→

  1581→def main():

  1794→
  1795→    args = parser.parse_args()
  1796→
  1797→    # ── Project auto-detection + layered config ──────────────────────
  1798→    if args.project:
  1799→        project_path = Path(args.project).resolve()
  1800→        project_name = project_path.name
  1801→    else:
  1802→        project_path, project_name = detect_project()
  1803→
  1804→    # Load layered config: defaults → ~/.hive/config.toml → .hive.toml → env
  1805→    Config.load(project_root=project_path)
  1806→
  1807→    # Ensure ~/.hive/ directory exists
  1808→    Config.HIVE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  1809→
  1810→    if args.command in ("setup", "init"):
  1811→        _do_setup(project_path, project_name, json_mode=args.json_mode)
  1812→        return
  1813→
  1814→    # Resolve DB path: CLI flag > config
  1815→    db_path = args.db or Config.DB_PATH
  1816→
  1817→    # Initialize database
  1818→    db = Database(db_path)
  1819→    db.connect()
  1820→
  1821→    # Create CLI
  1822→    cli = HiveCLI(db, str(project_path))
  1823→    json_mode = args.json_mode
  1824→
  1825→    try:
  1826→        if args.command == "create":
  1827→            cli.create(
  1828→                args.title,
  1829→                args.description,
  1830→                args.priority,
  1831→                args.issue_type,
  1832→                model=getattr(args, "model", None),
  1833→                tags=getattr(args, "tags", None),
  1834→                depends_on=getattr(args, "depends_on", None),
  1835→                json_mode=json_mode,
  1836→            )
  1837→
  1838→        elif args.command == "list":
  1839→            cli.list_issues(
  1840→                args.status,
  1841→                sort_by=args.sort,
  1842→                reverse=args.reverse,
  1843→                issue_type=args.issue_type,
  1844→                assignee=args.assignee,
  1845→                limit=args.limit,
  1846→                todo=args.todo,
  1847→                json_mode=json_mode,
  1848→            )
  1849→
  1850→        elif args.command == "show":
  1851→            show_json = json_mode or getattr(args, "show_format", "text") == "json"
  1852→            cli.show(args.issue_id, json_mode=show_json)
  1853→
  1854→        elif args.command == "review":
  1855→            cli.review(limit=args.limit, json_mode=json_mode)
  1856→
  1857→        elif args.command == "update":
  1858→            cli.update(
  1859→                args.issue_id,
  1860→                title=args.title,
  1861→                description=args.description,
  1862→                priority=args.priority,
  1863→                status=args.status,
  1864→                model=getattr(args, "model", None),
  1865→                tags=getattr(args, "tags", None),
  1866→                json_mode=json_mode,
  1867→            )
  1868→
  1869→        elif args.command == "cancel":
  1870→            cli.cancel(args.issue_id, reason=args.reason, json_mode=json_mode)
  1871→
  1872→        elif args.command == "finalize":
  1873→            cli.finalize(args.issue_id, resolution=args.resolution, json_mode=json_mode)
  1874→
  1875→        elif args.command == "retry":
  1876→            cli.retry(args.issue_id, notes=args.notes, json_mode=json_mode)
  1877→
  1878→        elif args.command == "epic":
  1879→            cli.epic(
  1880→                args.title,
  1881→                description=args.description,
